name: Ixtlilton
description: Ixtlilton, also known as Ixtlilton Tlacolteotl, was an Aztec deity associated with medicine, healing, and children. He was considered a benevolent god, particularly revered for his role in curing diseases and protecting the young. He was often invoked to heal illnesses, especially those affecting children. Ixtlilton was also associated with games, festivals, and celebrations, bringing joy and well-being to the Aztec people. He was seen as a protector against evil spirits and misfortune, offering solace and hope in times of adversity. Ixtlilton’s temples were places of healing and refuge, where priests would perform rituals and offer prayers to alleviate suffering. He was typically depicted with a black face and adorned with symbols of medicine and healing, reflecting his compassionate nature and dedication to the well-being of humanity. His role extended beyond just physical healing; he also provided emotional and spiritual support, fostering a sense of community and resilience among the Aztecs. Sacrifices were made to him, often of copal incense and other offerings, to maintain his favor and ensure continued health and prosperity for the community. He was a crucial figure in the Aztec pantheon, embodying the values of care, compassion, and the importance of protecting the vulnerable.
subtitle: God of Medicine, Healing, and Children

keyMyths.title: The Healing Touch
keyMyths.description: Ixtlilton is said to have healed a dying child with a touch of his hand, restoring their health and vitality. This act solidified his reputation as a powerful healer and protector of children.

keyMyths.title: Guardian of the Sick
keyMyths.description: During times of widespread illness, Ixtlilton was invoked to ward off disease and protect the vulnerable. His priests would perform elaborate rituals to appease him and ensure the health of the community.

keyMyths.title: The Joyful Celebrations
keyMyths.description: Festivals dedicated to Ixtlilton were filled with music, dancing, and games, reflecting his association with joy and well-being. These celebrations served to honor his benevolence and ensure continued prosperity.
